steps: Set up a 50 µL reaction as outlined below: %s %s

Set the thermal cycler for the program below: %s %s Spike in additional 1ul of SapI enzyme every 60 mins, to each sample At the 2:30 mark, add 1ul of rSap- this is to dephosphorylate the 5' end to re-ligate the ends.
Run all digested samples on a 0.5% TBE gel to ensure complete digestion of the backbone. Also run 1 µg of original starting template as a visual reference for the supercoiled plasmid.
Gel extract the linearized product.
Use the Clean & Concentrator kit on the gel extraction product. To avoid issues with freeze-thaws, aliquot the cleaned, linearized product in small volumes.
This adds the variant library inserts to the pre-barcoded vector using SapI. Set up reaction as outlined below:

Here we normally use 1:3 nmol backbone to each insert ratio, below is an example with a backbone and 4 insert frag. please note three of the fragments are on the helper plasmid, thus the calculation is done on the whole helper plasmid.
Here is a ligation calculator by NEB to use if you don't have a formula set up.
Here is NEB's general protocol for GG with SapI .
Neg Ctl: Using the vector with no insert. %s %s
Set a thermocycler for the following program: Spike in 1ul of SapI every hour. %s %s
Set up additional digestion reaction as follows: %s %s
Incubate at 37 °C for at least 02:00:00 , or Overnight . Spike in SapI every hour.
Purify the product with the Zymo Clean & Concentrator kit. Pool 2 sample reactions ( 40 µL ) for 4 columns.
Measure the final eluate with Nanodrop to look for a normal purity curve. 260/280 ratio should be:
Quantify the product concentration with Qubit BR assay. The final eluate should be >5ng/uL for the samples and very low for the negative control.
Prepare cells for transformation
Prepare for recovery
Electroporation
Sample recovery

Count colonies on dilution plates to calculate the approximate variant to barcode coverage For examples: Library X : 205 x 22 variant changes = 4510 aa variants. We want to have to pick the flask that produces between 45,100 -135,300 colonies. This indicates between 10-30x coverage over the variants. Best to average all three dilution plates for each bottleneck portion to ensure the most accurate estimation proceed to the maxi preps if the coverage is good. %s %s
Warm SOC to 37 °C .
Chill empty cuvettes On ice for 00:15:00 .
Thaw NEB electrocompetent cells (100 uL/tube) on ice for approximately 00:05:00 .

To chilled cuvettes, add 26 µL of thawed cells. Pipette to first stop only - do not create air bubbles as this will cause the electroporation to fail. Can use long gel-loading tips to do this more easily.

Add 1 µL - 2 µL of cleaned DNA from golden gate reaction and controls to the corresponding cuvette containing cells. Pipette to first stop only - do not create air bubbles. Do not add more than 3 uL of plasmid.
Incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .
Flick the cuvette gently, then lightly tap on the bench. Place the cuvette back On ice .
While the cells are incubating On ice , prepare glass recovery tubes with 900 µL SOC each, and warm to 37 °C .
Set the MicroPulser Electroporator to Manual for 2.0 ms. The electroporator is automatically set for 6 kV.
Prepare six overnight growth Erlenmeyer flasks with 300 mL of LB + 30 µL carbenicillin for each sample. Use 500 mL flasks for enough air circulation.

Label the LB + ampicillin dilution plates for each sample. Place in 37 °C after labeling to pre-warm. For Negative Golden Gate control, need 2 dilution plates For library and pUC19, need 3 dilution plates each.
Starting with the negative control cuvette, wipe the outside of the cuvette dry to remove condensation and ice.

Place the cuvette in the electroporator, ensuring the metal on the cuvette makes contact with the electrodes, and press the Pulse button. If the electroporator makes a loud noise and displays “Arc” on the digital readout, the sample has failed and is no longer good.
Remove the cuvette from the electroporator and place back On ice .
Repeat with all cuvettes prepared.
Remove the glass recovery tubes containing the SOC from 37 °C .
Transfer the cells from each cuvette to the glass recovery tubes.

From the warmed bottle of SOC, add 100 µL to wash the electroporation cuvette, then transfer from the cuvette to the corresponding glass recovery tube. Repeat this for all samples. Long gel-loading tips may be helpful for sample recovery from the cuvettes.
Place the glass recovery tubes into the shaking incubator for 01:00:00 at 37 °C .
Prepare dilution tubes and plates

As the cells are recovering, prepare microcentrifuge tubes for serial dilution of the library and controls. We recommend to start with a bottleneck scheme by dividing the 1ml recovery culture to 6 portions: 500 µL , 250 µL , 125 µL , 2x 50 µL , and 25 µL . For each portion, prepare 4 tubes (D0-D3) with the listed volume of LB. Label three plates (D1-D3) for each bottleneck portion (total of 18 plates). %s %s
For Puc, prepare two tubes and plates as the following Negative control and H 2 O. %s %s
For Negative control and H 2 O, plate 100 mL from the 1 mL recovery culture to the respective plates.

Prepare bottleneck for overnight growth and plating dilution plates. After the 1 hour recovery, add the appropriate portion(as below) from the 1 mL culture to the respective 500-mL flask based on the bottlenecking scheme. Place all 500 flasks in the 37 °C shaking incubator for 00:03:00 to ensure it is well mixed. No need to prepare overnight growth flasks for Puc19, negative control, and H 2 O controls. %s %s
Mix each dilution well. Plate each dilution according to the dilution scheme above. Incubate Overnight at 37 °C .
Incubate Overnight growth flasks in 30 °C for 14:00:00 - 16:00:00 .
creator: Sarah Heidl
